Private Travel Proposal · Prepared for the Family

A Rhine River Cruise for John & Carol

Fully-assisted, door-to-door · 12–19 August 2026 · Basel to Amsterdam

For review by Ellen  |  Based on Scenic’s live booking price for Balcony Suite 206; direct flights confirmed via Bristol.

This is a plan built around one promise: John and Carol are looked after from their own front door in Newport to their own front door again — with their luggage carried at every single transfer, and a wheelchair waiting for John at every airport. Below is the recommended cruise, the exact journey, what it includes, and what it costs.

The travellers, and what they need.
  • John (79) — an independent walker who paces himself; on long excursions he happily sits and waits. He needs a wheelchair only at airports, where luggage and walking distances combine. He cannot manage a suitcase himself.
  • Carol (77) — genuinely mobile (walks several miles a day), but cannot carry the cases and assist John at the same time.
  • The core requirement — every bag handled for them at every step, and a wheelchair on hand at each airport. A ship with a lift; gentle, optional excursions. Everything possible made effortless.

The Recommendation

Our pick   Scenic “A taste of the Rhine – A wine cruise” — an 8-day, 7-night all-inclusive luxury cruise from Basel to Amsterdam aboard the 163-guest, all-suite Scenic Pearl, departing Wednesday 12 August 2026.

It is the same ship, route and ports as Scenic’s classic “Rhine Highlights” — sailed downstream (Basel→Amsterdam) with a gentle wine theme layered on: vineyard visits and tastings alongside the usual sightseeing. Scenic is simply the best fit for John and Carol — an all-suite ship with a lift and a limited-mobility suite by reception, everything genuinely all-inclusive onboard, and graded excursions so they set their own pace. We arrange the direct flights and the assisted transfers around it.

Why the 12 August sailing. It’s the best-value week of the summer — Scenic’s current sale brings a Balcony Suite for the two of them to £5,790 — and an August trip keeps September free for Carol’s brother Brian’s 80th in Jesolo. One thing we plan around: the direct Bristol–Basel flight runs only on set days, so they fly out a day or two ahead and settle into Basel before boarding — a restful start, not a rushed one.

The Ship & Your Suite

Scenic Pearl is a 163-guest, all-suite “Space-Ship” — small, calm and beautifully finished, with a lift between all interior decks and a limited-mobility suite by reception. Every stateroom is a suite with a butler; the Balcony Suite has Scenic’s signature full-width “Sun Lounge” window that lowers at the touch of a button.

The cabin to reserve: a Balcony Suite on the middle (Sapphire) Deck — category B, the full 205 sq ft suite with the Sun Lounge. The main restaurant is on this same deck, so it’s the least-walking deck on the ship. Two equally good spots (same suite, same price): cabin 206, just steps from the restaurant, or 209–213, right beside the lift. (A Junior Balcony Suite, category BJ, is the roomier step-up; avoid the Jewel-deck Standard Suites — no balcony, only a small fixed window.)

Ship & suite photographs © Scenic (official). Shown for illustration; the exact suite is confirmed on booking.

The Journey — Door to Door

Every transfer is assisted and every bag is carried for them. John gets a wheelchair at each airport through free “special assistance.” We book both flights direct, so there are no connections to catch.

  1. Collected at the Newport front door by a private car we arrange, and driven to Bristol Airport (~45–60 min). The driver loads and handles all the luggage.
  2. Direct easyJet flight, Bristol → Basel (~1h40, nonstop). Free airport assistance provides John’s wheelchair and takes them both, with bags, through check-in, security and the gate — and again on arrival.
  3. A night or two in Basel at a lift-equipped riverside hotel — so they arrive rested and unhurried, and to match the direct-flight day. A short assisted transfer takes them to the ship.
  4. Embark Scenic Pearl in Basel. Porters take the cases straight to their suite; John walks the gangway with crew on hand.
  5. Seven nights down the Rhine — Strasbourg & Alsace, Baden-Baden, Heidelberg & the vineyards, Koblenz and the castle-lined Rhine Gorge, Cologne — then Amsterdam (full day-by-day below).
  6. Disembark in Amsterdam, then a direct KLM or easyJet flight, Amsterdam → Bristol (~1h20, many daily), airport assistance again — and a private car home to their Newport door.

The Route

Seven nights down the Rhine, Basel to Amsterdam — through the Rhineland and the Netherlands, with the castle-lined UNESCO gorge at its heart. Numbers mark the day of the cruise.

The Places You’ll See

Destination photos via Wikimedia Commons: Amsterdam © Basile Morin; Cologne © Ahgee; Marksburg/Rhine — Gary Todd (CC0); Heidelberg © Mikheil Baramidze; Baden-Baden © Franzfoto; Strasbourg © Dietmar Rabich; Basel © Matti Blume. Licensed CC BY-SA / CC0.

On the Rhine — Day by Day

Seven nights, Basel to Amsterdam. Each port offers a choice of included excursions (Scenic’s “Freechoice”), graded by effort, plus a signature “Enrich” evening — a private classical concert in a baroque palace — and, on this sailing, wine tastings and a vineyard visit woven through. The last column is written for John: which days he can stroll off the ship, which involve a coach, and which are worth taking gently.

Easy dockside / low-effort   Coach a bus ride to the sight   Take gently cobbles, hills or long walking

DayPort & sailingIncluded excursions (effort)Walk or coach?For John
1
Wed		 Basel (Switzerland) — embark afternoon; overnight in port Board & settle in; welcome reception aboard (Relaxed) Dockside Easy Settle in aboard, rested after the Basel stay.
2
Thu		 Kehl (for Strasbourg, France) & Alsace — sail on in the evening Strasbourg walking tour — Cathedral, Petite France (Active); Alsace wine village + cellar tasting (Moderate, wine); e-bike city tour (Active) Coach short ~15 min across the Rhine into Strasbourg Take gently Strasbourg is flat but cobbled and a fair walk; the Alsace wine-village + cellar tasting is the gentler, more seated choice.
3
Fri		 Rastatt / Plittersdorf (for Baden-Baden) — day ashore Baden-Baden spa-town walk (Relaxed); Caracalla thermal baths (Relaxed); Schloss Favorite palace (Moderate). Evening Scenic Enrich: private concert, Rastatt Baroque Palace, with a sparkling-wine reception (Relaxed, seated) Coach Baden-Baden is ~20 km inland Easy A gentle coach day — Baden-Baden is flat and elegant; the thermal-spa option is very low-effort, and the evening palace concert is all seated.
4
Sat		 Mannheim (for Heidelberg & the Pfalz vineyards) — day ashore Heidelberg orientation walk (Moderate); Heidelberg + Castle (Moderate); Fritz Ritter vineyard tour & Riesling tasting in the Pfalz (Moderate, wine) Coach Heidelberg ~20 km / 30–40 min; the vineyard a similar coach ride Take gently Heidelberg has cobbles and an uphill castle — take the orientation tour, or the town at his own pace. The vineyard tour & tasting is the gentler, mostly-seated alternative.
5
Sun		 Koblenz + Rhine Gorge scenic cruising — sail the UNESCO gorge of castles Koblenz old-town walk (Moderate); Koblenz Wine Museum (Relaxed); Rhine Gorge cruise with a regional-wine tasting & talk on deck (Relaxed, seated) Koblenz walkable (flat riverside); the gorge is enjoyed aboard Easy The loveliest afternoon of the week — seated on the sun deck as the castles pass, glass of Riesling in hand.
6
Mon		 Cologne (Köln) — arrive; evening free; sail overnight to Amsterdam Old Town + Cathedral walking tour (Moderate); Farina fragrance museum (Relaxed) Walkable — ship moors by the old town, ~5–10 min flat to the cathedral Easy A gentle dockside day; old-town squares are cobbled (skip the tower climb).
7
Tue		 Amsterdam — arrive; overnight in port Canal cruise (Relaxed); Fabrique des Lumières digital-art show (Relaxed); old-city walking tour (Moderate) Dockside / very short transfer Easy The canal cruise or light show are seated and a lovely way to finish.
8
Wed		 Amsterdam — disembark after breakfast; direct flight home from Amsterdam — (disembarkation) Transfer to Amsterdam (Schiphol) airport Easy Relaxed departure — the direct flight home runs most days.

The gist for John: the Rhine-Gorge afternoon (Day 5), Cologne (Day 6), Amsterdam (Day 7) and the Baden-Baden spa day (Day 3) are his easy ones. Strasbourg (Day 2) and Heidelberg (Day 4) involve the most walking — and both have a gentler, mostly-seated wine-themed alternative he can take instead. Nothing is compulsory.

Note: This is Scenic’s wine-themed “A taste of the Rhine” sailing (Basel→Amsterdam) — the same ports as the standard Rhine Highlights, with wine tastings and a vineyard visit added. Exact arrival, departure and sailing times are confirmed in the final Scenic documents; excursion menus can vary slightly by departure.

What’s Included, What We Book, What’s Not

The most important thing to be clear about — what Scenic’s fare covers, what we arrange around it, and what isn’t included. In plain terms:

Included in the Scenic fare (£5,790 the two)

  • ✓ 7-night cruise, Balcony Suite 206 (Sun Lounge)
  • ✓ Butler service; all meals aboard
  • ✓ All drinks all day (incl. spirits & the wine tastings)
  • ✓ Every excursion — sightseeing & wine
  • ✓ All onboard tips & gratuities
  • ✓ Private palace concert; Wi-Fi; laundry
  • ✓ Up to £400pp toward flights (air credit)

What we book around it

  • • Direct flights — easyJet Bristol→Basel out, KLM/easyJet Amsterdam→Bristol home
  • • Private car, Newport ↔ Bristol airport (both legs)
  • • 1–2 nights in Basel before boarding (rested start)
  • • Short airport↔ship transfers (Basel & Amsterdam)
  • • Airport special assistance for John (free; we pre-book)
  • • Travel insurance — likely their existing annual policy

Not included / not needed: Scenic’s own flights (we self-book to guarantee direct) and their airport–ship coach (that only applies to Scenic-booked flights, so we arrange short private transfers); no wheelchair to buy (airport assistance provides one). Why self-book the flights? Scenic’s included flights “may not be a direct service” — they can route via a hub. Booking the easyJet/KLM nonstop ourselves is the only way to guarantee no connections; we’d take Scenic’s no-fly fare (the £400pp air credit comes off) so the net cost works out about the same.

The Budget — all-in for the pair

ItemCost
Scenic cruise — Balcony Suite 206, 12 Aug (all-inclusive onboard + flights via £400pp air credit; live sale price)£5,790
Direct-flight adjustment & airport↔ship transfers (net, if we self-book nonstop on Scenic’s no-fly fare — roughly a wash)£0–500
Private car, Newport ↔ Bristol airport (both legs)£210–320
Basel hotel, 1–2 nights before boarding£250–500
Airport wheelchair assistance for JohnFree
Travel insurance (likely their existing annual policy; possible top-up)£0–400
Total, the pair — true door-to-door≈ £6,300–7,500

About a third of the £13–18k budget — leaving ample room to step them up to a roomier Junior Balcony Suite (BJ) or add extra nights, and still be comfortably inside budget.

On the price. The £5,790 is Scenic’s live booking price for cabin 206 under the current Summer Sale (Save £2,600pp), flights via a £400pp air credit — not an estimate. Because August is inside Scenic’s final-payment window, a booking is payable in full at the time rather than by deposit — so this is about securing the cabin, not chasing a deadline.

Details to Confirm at Booking

  • Cabin 206 (Balcony Suite, Cat B) on the 12 Aug wine cruise — and whether to book fly-inclusive or the no-fly fare (so we self-book the direct flights). Scenic UK: 0161 768 8129.
  • The direct easyJet Bristol–Basel operating days in August (it runs ~twice weekly) — confirm on easyjet.com and align the Basel arrival, then book the seats early.
  • Two quick checks with John & Carol’s insurer: that the cancellation limit covers the full trip value and that cruise cover is included.

Honest Notes

  • The make-or-break, and it clears: a direct Bristol–Basel (easyJet) and direct Amsterdam–Bristol (KLM/easyJet) both exist for August 2026. Cardiff has no direct Basel flight, so we use Bristol. The one thing to manage: the Bristol–Basel flight runs ~twice a week, so they fly out a day or two early — hence the Basel hotel nights.
  • The price is real, not an estimate — £5,790 is Scenic’s live booking price for cabin 206 on 12 Aug. The door-to-door extras around it are costed ranges; the exact flight and hotel figures firm up when we book.
  • River berths in Basel and Amsterdam are assigned shortly before sailing; occasionally a ship moors a short drive out. Our transfers absorb this either way.
  • The open-air sun deck is reached by stairs on essentially every river ship (they sit low to pass under bridges). The interior lift serves all cabin and dining decks — no stairs between their suite, the restaurant and reception.

Next step: confirm cabin 206 on the 12 August sailing and the direct-flight days, then book. From there this becomes a final day-by-day for John and Carol, every time confirmed.

Prepared as a private, word-of-mouth service — no booking has been made and no deposit taken. Everything here is for the family’s review.
